reasoning: While the LLM fidelity scores are extremely high and indicate strong content preservation, programmatic detectors identified pervasive retention of header/footer elements across all 5 pages. This is classified as a CRITICAL defect because screen readers will encounter duplicate, disruptive artifacts on every page. Additionally, a major structural issue with mixed list numbering (flattening) was identified. Due to the presence of a critical defect, this document must be rejected.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 5; Page 3 of 5
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

I checked for instances where structural emphasis, particularly underlining, was lost in the output. In the source PDF on pages 3 and 4, the introductory phrase for every bullet point in both lists (e.g., 'Beneficial Reuse Areas', 'Costs', 'Dam Safety') is heavily emphasized using an underline (and bold). The output JSON completely drops this underline/emphasis formatting, rendering them as plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Beneficial Reuse Areas – There was a concern about contamination resulting from off-site fill areas. [Note: 'Beneficial Reuse Areas' is explicitly underlined in the source PDF]
concerns.output_quote: "Beneficial Reuse Areas – There was a concern about contamination resulting from off-site fill areas."

action_items: Update pipeline to properly exclude header/footer artifacts from the rendered HTML
Correct the list structure to properly nest sub-points instead of flattening them into mixed numbering at the same level
Ensure underline and bold emphasis formatting is retained in the output
